classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: John of Damascus: Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.

metadata.author: John of Damascus: Exposition of the Orthodox Faith

ccel.title: NPNF2-09. Hilary of Poitiers, John of Damascus

source.title: NPNF2-09. Hilary of Poitiers, John of Damascus

title: Concerning angels.

source.title: NPNF2-09. Hilary of Poitiers, John of Damascus

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: He is Himself the Maker and Creator of the angels: for He brought them out of nothing into being and created them after His own image, an incorporeal race, a sort of spirit or immaterial fire: in the words of the divine David, He maketh His angels spirits, and His ministers a flame of fire: and He has described their lightness and the ardour, and heat, and keenness and sharpness with which they hunger for God and serve Him, and how they are borne to the regions above and are quite delivered from all material thought.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: An angel, then, is an intelligent essence, in perpetual motion, with free-will, incorporeal, ministering to God, having obtained by grace an immortal nature: and the Creator alone knows the form and limitation of its essence.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: But all that we can understand is, that it is incorporeal and immaterial.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For all that is compared with God Who alone is incomparable, we find to be dense and material.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For in reality only the Deity is immaterial and incorporeal.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: The angel’s nature then is rational, and intelligent, and endowed with free-will, changeable in will, or fickle.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For all that is created is changeable, and only that which is un-created is unchangeable.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Also all that is rational is endowed with free-will.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: As it is, then, rational and intelligent, it is endowed with free-will: and as it is created, it is changeable, having power either to abide or progress in goodness, or to turn towards evil.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: It is not susceptible of repentance because it is incorporeal.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For it is owing to the weakness of his body that man comes to have repentance.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: It is immortal, not by nature but by grace.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For all that has had beginning comes also to its natural end.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: But God alone is eternal, or rather, He is above the Eternal: for He, the Creator of times, is not under the dominion of time, but above time.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: They are secondary intelligent lights derived from that first light which is without beginning, for they have the power of illumination; they have no need of tongue or hearing, but without uttering words they communicate to each other their own thoughts and counsels.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Through the Word, therefore, all the angels were created, and through the sanctification by the Holy Spirit were they brought to perfection, sharing each in proportion to his worth and rank in brightness and grace.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: They are circumscribed: for when they are in the Heaven they are not on the earth: and when they are sent by God down to the earth they do not remain in the Heaven.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: They are not hemmed in by walls and doors, and bars and seals, for they are quite unlimited.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Unlimited, I repeat, for it is not as they really are that they reveal themselves to the worthy men to whom God wishes them to appear, but in a changed form which the beholders are capable of seeing.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For that alone is naturally and strictly unlimited which is un-created.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For every created thing is limited by God Who created it.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Further, apart from their essence they receive the sanctification from the Spirit: through the divine grace they prophesy: they have no need of marriage for they are immortal.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Seeing that they are minds they are in mental places, and are not circumscribed after the fashion of a body.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For they have not a bodily form by nature, nor are they extended in three dimensions.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: But to whatever post they may be assigned, there they are present after the manner of a mind and energise, and cannot be present and energise in various places at the same time.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Whether they are equals in essence or differ from one another we know not.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: God, their Creator, Who knoweth all things, alone knoweth.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: But they differ from each other in brightness and position, whether it is that their position is dependent on their brightness, or their brightness on their position: and they impart brightness to one another, because they excel one another in rank and nature.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: And clearly the higher share their brightness and knowledge with the lower.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: They are mighty and prompt to fulfil the will of the Deity, and their nature is endowed with such celerity that wherever the Divine glance bids them there they are straightway found.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: They are the guardians of the divisions of the earth: they are set over nations and regions, allotted to them by their Creator: they govern all our affairs and bring us succour.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: And the reason surely is because they are set over us by the divine will and command and are ever in the vicinity of God.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: With difficulty they are moved to evil, yet they are not absolutely immoveable: but now they are altogether immoveable, not by nature but by grace and by their nearness to the Only Good.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: They behold God according to their capacity, and this is their food.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: They are above us for they are incorporeal, and are free of all bodily passion, yet are not passionless: for the Deity alone is passionless.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: They take different forms at the bidding of their Master, God, and thus reveal themselves to men and unveil the divine mysteries to them.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: They have Heaven for their dwelling-place, and have one duty, to sing God’s praise and carry out His divine will.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Moreover, as that most holy, and sacred, and gifted theologian, Dionysius the Areopagite, says, All theology, that is to say, the holy Scripture, has nine different names for the heavenly essences.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: These essences that divine master in sacred things divides into three groups, each containing three.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: And the first group, he says, consists of those who are in God’s presence and are said to be directly and immediately one with Him, viz., the Seraphim with their six wings, the many-eyed Cherubim and those that sit in the holiest thrones.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: The second group is that of the Dominions, and the Powers, and the Authorities; and the third, and last, is that of the Rulers and Archangels and Angels.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Some, indeed, like Gregory the Theologian, say that these were before the creation of other things.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: He thinks that the angelic and heavenly powers were first and that thought was their function.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Others, again, hold that they were created after the first heaven was made.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: But all are agreed that it was before the foundation of man.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For myself, I am in harmony with the theologian.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For it was fitting that the mental essence should be the first created, and then that which can be perceived, and finally man himself, in whose being both parts are united.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: But those who say that the angels are creators of any kind of essence whatever are the mouth of their father, the devil.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For since they are created things they are not creators.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: But He Who creates and provides for and maintains all things is God, Who alone is uncreate and is praised and glorified in the Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit.
